name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
description: A factor-tilted, globally diversified portfolio designed to beat VT on a risk-adjusted basis by overweighting US quality/growth, adding selective international exposure, and including a modest defensive allocation via dividend growth and short-duration bonds. The tilt away from market-cap-weight VT is deliberate — capturing small-cap, dividend growth, and international re-rating tailwinds that a plain VT holding misses.

name: ChatGPT 5.4
description: This portfolio is designed to outperform a pure global-market-cap benchmark such as VT over the long run by maintaining broad global diversification while adding targeted tilts toward U.S. small-cap value (AVUV) and quality stocks (QUAL), two factors that have historically delivered excess returns over long periods. A modest allocation to real estate (VNQ) and bonds (BND) aims to improve diversification and reduce portfolio volatility without materially sacrificing expected returns.

name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
description: This strategy aims to outperform the broad global benchmark (VT) by constructing a foundational, low-cost international core via VTI, VEA, and VWO, while aggressively introducing targeted factor tilts. It overweights US small-cap value via AVUV to capture structurally persistent size and value premiums, while balancing it with large-cap secular growth through QQQM and high-quality, cash-flow-rich companies via SCHD. This blended multi-factor overlay optimizes the portfolio for risk-adjusted alpha over the next quarter without entirely sacrificing the downside-mitigation benefits of global diversification.

name: DeepSeek R1
description: This portfolio seeks to outpace the cap-weighted global benchmark VT by overweighting small-cap value (via AVUV and AVDV) and adding a dedicated real estate sleeve (VNQ), factors historically associated with higher long-term expected returns. It maintains broad geographic diversification across US, developed international, and emerging markets to stay relatively benchmark-aware while aiming for enhanced risk‑adjusted performance.

name: Perplexity
description: This is a low-cost core-satellite portfolio that stays broadly diversified while tilting slightly toward U.S. equities, mid/small caps, and a modest bond sleeve for volatility control. It uses only U.S.-listed ETF tickers that are available on Yahoo Finance, and it keeps the structure simple enough to track against VT over a single quarter while still allowing some active positioning.
